nodes.label: ADR 0002: Detect divergence by content, not ancestry
nodes.norm_label: adr 0002: detect divergence by content, not ancestry

nodes.label: Rationale: squash/rebase merges rewrite SHAs; ancestry-only detection leaves edges permanently diverged and PR creation fails with HTTP 422; a private state database would violate the no-control-plane posture
nodes.norm_label: rationale: squash/rebase merges rewrite shas; ancestry-only detection leaves edges permanently diverged and pr creation fails with http 422; a private state database would violate the no-control-plane posture

nodes.label: ADR 0003: Read configuration from a pinned ref
nodes.norm_label: adr 0003: read configuration from a pinned ref

nodes.label: Rationale: config is itself promoted and differs per branch; reading the triggering ref is nondeterministic and lets untrusted PR config run with write credentials
nodes.norm_label: rationale: config is itself promoted and differs per branch; reading the triggering ref is nondeterministic and lets untrusted pr config run with write credentials

nodes.label: Design proposal in skaphos-resources under tools/oiax/
nodes.norm_label: design proposal in skaphos-resources under tools/oiax/

nodes.label: Pure Provider-Neutral Engine
nodes.source_location: Invariants that must not be violated
nodes.rationale: Keeping internal/engine free of Git and forge dependencies makes plans deterministic and provider-neutral.

nodes.label: Pinned Declarative Configuration
nodes.source_location: Invariants that must not be violated
nodes.rationale: Reading declarative configuration from a pinned ref prevents triggering-ref changes from controlling privileged behavior.

nodes.label: Metadata-Based Managed Request Identity
nodes.source_location: Invariants that must not be violated
nodes.rationale: Body metadata and branch relationships are stable management signals; titles are not.

nodes.label: Release PR Label Reconciliation
nodes.rationale: Changing merged release PRs from autorelease: pending to autorelease: tagged prevents subsequent release-please runs from jamming on outstanding untagged PRs.

nodes.label: Annotated Immutable SemVer Tag
nodes.source_location: Create release tag after release PR merge

nodes.label: Release Tag Monotonicity Guard
nodes.rationale: Only advancing the floating major tag for the newest immutable release prevents reruns of older releases from rolling consumers backward.

nodes.label: Declared Promotion Graph
nodes.rationale: A declarative graph lets Oiax ensure one active managed request per diverged edge while leaving merge and deployment decisions to existing controls.

nodes.label: Backflow
nodes.rationale: Downstream hotfixes are returned to the authoritative source branch so they re-enter forward branch promotion.

nodes.label: Oiax Compatibility Contracts
nodes.rationale: Exit codes, plan JSON, managed metadata, and pkg/api require ADR-governed evolution because external automation depends on them.

nodes.label: Immutable ADR Policy
nodes.rationale: Significant hard-to-reverse decisions are recorded immutably and superseded by new ADRs so architectural history remains auditable.

nodes.label: Branch-per-Environment GitOps
nodes.rationale: Long-lived environment branches use adjacent pull requests for promotion, but manual requests go stale and can lose downstream hotfixes.

nodes.label: Content Equivalence Ladder
nodes.rationale: Full-history content equivalence avoids spurious promotion requests after SHA-rewriting squash or rebase merges.

nodes.label: Human and Repository Policy Gates
nodes.rationale: Oiax creates promotion requests but leaves safety decisions to branch protection, required checks, CODEOWNERS, and human review.

nodes.label: Full-History Checkout
nodes.rationale: fetch-depth: 0 preserves merge bases required by patch-identity and baseline equivalence checks.

nodes.label: GitHub App Installation Token
nodes.rationale: A GitHub App token allows managed pull requests to trigger required CI without write-user approval that default GITHUB_TOKEN events may require.

nodes.label: GitHub Marketplace Listing
nodes.rationale: Marketplace publishing is a one-time manual discoverability step because GitHub exposes no API for its release-form toggle.

nodes.label: Immutable Release Marketplace Constraint
nodes.rationale: The Marketplace checkbox must be set while a release is editable or draft because publication locks immutable releases.

nodes.label: Action Pinned Config Ref
nodes.author: Skaphos
nodes.rationale: Defaulting configuration to the repository default branch deliberately ignores potentially untrusted triggering-ref configuration.

nodes.label: Adopt the Name Oiax
nodes.rationale: Oiax preserves the tiller metaphor and Greek Skaphos naming register without colliding in the Kubernetes/CD ecosystem.

nodes.label: Tiller Ecosystem Collision
nodes.rationale: The working name Tiller collided with Helm v2's component in the exact ecosystem the tool serves.

nodes.label: Human-in-the-Loop Steering
nodes.rationale: The tiller metaphor communicates that Oiax removes workflow toil without removing human gates.

nodes.label: Content-Based Divergence Detection
nodes.rationale: An ordered content equivalence ladder supports merge, rebase, and squash histories without an external state database.

nodes.label: Ancestry-Only Detection Failure
nodes.rationale: Squash and rebase rewrite SHAs, making ancestry-only comparison permanently report divergence and provoking forge HTTP 422 errors.

nodes.label: Reachability-to-Baseline Equivalence Ladder
nodes.rationale: The first conclusive result among reachability, stable patch identity, head-tree equality, and sourceHead baseline determines equivalence.

nodes.label: sourceHead Promotion Baseline
nodes.rationale: Merged managed-request metadata durably records what source head was promoted while Git remains the content source of truth.

nodes.label: Single Pinned Configuration Ref
nodes.rationale: Exactly one pinned ref makes configuration behavior deterministic across events and isolates privileged execution from untrusted pull-request configuration.

nodes.label: Triggering-Ref Configuration Risk
nodes.rationale: Reading whichever ref triggered the run is nondeterministic and can expose write credentials to untrusted configuration.

nodes.label: Configuration Security Boundary
nodes.rationale: Ignoring all non-pinned refs ensures proposed configuration is never executed with privileged credentials.

nodes.label: Backflow Execution
nodes.rationale: Downstream-only commits are replayed to one authoritative source branch using deterministic, convergent Git and forge state.

nodes.label: Deterministic Backflow Return Branch
nodes.rationale: A fixed-length source-head-keyed oiax/backflow branch makes repeated and racing runs converge while a new hotfix creates a distinct reviewable request.

nodes.label: Event-Driven Concurrency Without Locks
nodes.rationale: Correctness depends on deterministic state and ancestry guards rather than event ordering or locks because runs can overlap, duplicate, or be missed.

nodes.label: Backflow Identity Ladder
nodes.rationale: Cherry-pick provenance, stable patch identity, and a manual skip trailer determine whether downstream content is already represented on target.

nodes.label: Oiax-Backflow Skip Trailer
nodes.rationale: A commit-local manual escape hatch suppresses intentional downstream-only changes without introducing an Oiax state database.

nodes.label: Ephemeral Detached Worktree Replay
nodes.rationale: Detached temporary worktrees isolate cherry-picks from the caller's checkout and index and are cleaned on every exit path.

nodes.label: Backflow Conflict as Reported Divergence
nodes.rationale: A genuine content conflict produces exit 3 with actionable commit context and no push; operational failures remain ordinary errors.

nodes.label: Supersede Stale Backflow Request
nodes.source_location: Execution mechanics: At most one active request
nodes.rationale: A newer source head closes only ancestry-proven stale managed requests and opens a new request rather than rewriting already-reviewed history.

nodes.label: Canonical Config API v1
nodes.rationale: The stable v1 string matches the 1.0 compatibility promise while preserving the settled PromotionGraph shape.

nodes.label: Deprecated v1alpha1 Configuration Alias
nodes.rationale: Accepting the old string with one warning avoids breaking existing YAML for a label-only migration with no behavioral change.

nodes.label: pkg/api/v1 Go Import Path
nodes.rationale: The one-time breaking import-path move at the 1.0 boundary aligns Go API naming with the stable configuration contract.

nodes.label: Pure Config Parse Function
nodes.rationale: Keeping config.Parse as byte-to-struct with no I/O preserves composability and leaves user-facing deprecation warnings to the CLI.

links.source_location: build plan step of the loop
edge evaluation will live in internal/engine
design proposal lives in skaphos-resources under tools/oiax/ but is outside this repository
both reconcile environment promotion over Git branches; gitops-promoter is a Kubernetes controller, Oiax is CI-native
the hotfix-return flow is backflow, never reconciliation
backflow identity check reuses the same posture
the same external tool appears as prior-art inspiration and as this repo's release automation
Action downloads the binary and verifies against checksums.txt
the tag pushed by release-please triggers GoReleaser
